story.iWant: to implement model selection rules based on task complexity
story.soThat: I can route tasks to optimal models và achieve 40-50% cost reduction với acceptable quality trade-off

subtasks.subtask: Map complexity levels to models: - simple → gpt-4o-mini, qwen3-30b (cheap models) - medium → deepseek-v3-1, claude-haiku-4-5 (balanced models) - complex → qwen3-235b (powerful but cheaper) - very_complex → gpt-4o, claude-sonnet (premium models)
Test routing rules với sample tasks
Test ModelRouter class với sample tasks
Apply routing rules to select model

Handle multiple models matching same complexity level: - Prefer cheaper model if multiple models match (e.g., simple → prefer gpt-4o-mini over qwen3-30b if both available) - Consider model availability (check if model is enabled và accessible) - Consider model capabilities (if task requires specific features like vision, prefer model with those capabilities) - Use round-robin hoặc random selection if all factors equal
Handle edge cases (unknown complexity, model unavailable)
Test selection logic với different complexity levels
Track routing metrics (model distribution, routing accuracy)
Add routing metrics to monitoring dashboard
Add fallback logic nếu selected model fails
Fallback to next best model based on complexity
Test fallback mechanism với failure scenarios
Track cost per request (before vs after routing)
Add cost metrics to monitoring dashboard
Test cost tracking với sample requests

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Routing rules được defined để map complexity levels to models
Model selection logic được implemented based on complexity
Routing decisions được logged và monitored
Fallback mechanism được implemented nếu selected model fails
Cost savings được measured và tracked

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Epic Breakdown: LLM Optimization
doc.section: Epic 3: Multi-Model Orchestration - Story 3.2: Model Selection Rules
doc.snippet: Story 3.2 defines requirements for implementing model selection rules. Expected savings: $36-45/month (40-50% cost reduction). Quality Impact: ⚠️ 10-15% (cheaper models for simple tasks, acceptable trade-off). Effort: 2 hours. Prerequisites: Story 3.1 (Task Complexity Classification), Story 1.4 (Dual-mode architecture).

doc.title: ChainLens AI Worker - Master Optimization Plan v1.1
doc.section: Phase 3: Multi-Model Orchestration - Task 3.2: Model Selection Rules
doc.snippet: Model selection rules map complexity levels to optimal models: simple → cheap models (gpt-4o-mini, qwen3-30b), medium → balanced models (deepseek-v3-1, claude-haiku-4-5), complex → powerful but cheaper (qwen3-235b), very_complex → premium models (gpt-4o, claude-sonnet). Quality impact: 10-15% (acceptable trade-off).

doc.title: Research Guidance: Multi-Model Orchestration
doc.snippet: Model selection based on task complexity enables 40-50% cost reduction với acceptable quality trade-off (80-85%). Routing rules map complexity to optimal models. Fallback mechanism required if selected model fails.

file.reason: New file to create. Implement ModelRouter class với routing logic, routing rules, model selection, fallback mechanism, và cost tracking.
TaskClassifier from Story 3.1. Provides complexity classification results (complexity level, confidence score) for routing decisions.
Agent execution flow. Need to integrate routing vào agent execution flow to select optimal model based on complexity before LLM calls.
LLM API call handler. Use routed model from ModelRouter for LLM calls instead of default model selection.
Current model selection logic. ModelRouter will override this based on complexity classification.
Model registry với available models và capabilities. ModelRouter needs to check model availability và capabilities for routing decisions.
Configuration management. Can add routing configuration (routing rules, fallback settings) here.

constraint.description: Acceptable quality trade-off (80-85%) for 40-50% cost savings. Cheaper models for simple tasks, premium models for complex tasks.
Dual-mode architecture: Original mode (baseline) + Optimized mode (with routing). Feature flags allow switching and rollback.
Routing rules map complexity levels to models: simple → gpt-4o-mini, qwen3-30b; medium → deepseek-v3-1, claude-haiku-4-5; complex → qwen3-235b; very_complex → gpt-4o, claude-sonnet.
If multiple models match same complexity level: prefer cheaper model, consider availability, consider capabilities, use round-robin/random if all factors equal.
If selected model fails, fallback to next best model based on complexity. Log fallback events.
Track cost per request (before vs after routing). Calculate cost savings percentage. Target: 40-50% cost reduction.
Must not break existing LLM calls. Routing should be transparent to callers. Test with existing agent configurations.

interface.name: ModelRouter class
interface.signature: class ModelRouter: def __init__(self, task_classifier: TaskClassifier, model_registry: ModelRegistry): ... async def route(self, task: str, user_id: str) -> str: ... # Returns model_id

interface.name: TaskClassifier.classify()
interface.signature: async def classify(self, task: str) -> Dict[str, Any]: ... # Returns {"complexity": "simple|medium|complex|very_complex", "confidence": float}

interface.name: ModelRegistry.get()
interface.signature: def get(self, model_id: str) -> Optional[Model]: ... # Returns Model object với pricing, capabilities, enabled status

interface.name: make_llm_api_call()
interface.signature: async def make_llm_api_call(messages: List[Dict[str, Any]], model_name: str, ...) -> Union[Dict[str, Any], AsyncGenerator, ModelResponse]

tests.standards: Testing standards include: Quality validation (routing decisions correct, quality impact 80-85%), Performance testing (routing overhead <50ms, cost savings 40-50%), Integration testing (TaskClassifier integration, existing LLM calls, different models, agent configurations, fallback scenarios). Monitor quality impact và cost savings.

test.idea: Unit test: Verify ModelRouter class creation và initialization
Integration test: Test ModelRouter class với TaskClassifier
Unit test: Verify routing rules (complexity levels mapped to models)
Integration test: Test routing decisions với sample tasks
Unit test: Verify model selection logic (prefer cheaper, consider availability, consider capabilities)
Integration test: Test selection logic với different complexity levels và scenarios
Unit test: Verify logging logic (routing decisions, metrics tracking)
Integration test: Verify routing metrics are logged và appear in monitoring dashboard
Unit test: Verify fallback logic (fallback to next best model if selected model fails)
Integration test: Test fallback mechanism với failure scenarios
Unit test: Verify cost calculation (cost per request, cost savings percentage)
Integration test: Verify cost tracking và cost savings measurement (target: 40-50%)
